data.content: Here's what changes with MSTest.Sdk — it replaces the `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` project SDK and eliminates all three MSTest package references:

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert from the traditional MSTest v2 setup (with separate `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` packages) to use the `MSTest.Sdk` project SDK.

With MSTest.Sdk, you replace `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `MSTest.Sdk` and remove the three MSTest-related package references. The SDK handles all of that automatically.

Let me convert the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content: The build succeeds. The 2 test failures are due to the newer MSTest being stricter about `DataRow` argument types — `1L`/`2L` are `long` but the parameter is `int`. Let me fix that pre-existing mismatch that the upgrade now enforces:
data.reasoningText:
The 2 test failures are a pre-existing bug in the test code — `DataRow(1L, ...)` passes `long` but the parameter is `int`. This was already broken before my change (MSTest.Sdk just enforces it more strictly now). This is not related to my conversion — it's a pre-existing type mismatch. The 3 other tests pass fine. The conversion itself is correct.

Let me note this to the user but not fix the pre-existing test bug since it's unrelated to the SDK conversion.

Actually, looking more carefully, the old version (MSTest 2.2.10) was more lenient and would have silently converted `long` to `int`. The new version (3.8.3) is stricter. This test failure is caused by the upgrade, so I should fix it as part of this conversion.


I'll correct the DataRow parameters to use the proper int type instead of long.

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of what changed:

**`TestProject.csproj`** — switched SDK from `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` → `MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3` and removed the three package references (`MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`). The SDK bundles all of them. `IsPackable` was also removed since MSTest.Sdk sets it to `false` by default.

**`UserServiceTests.cs`** — changed `DataRow(1L, ...)` / `DataRow(2L, ...)` to `DataRow(1, ...)` / `DataRow(2, ...)` because the newer MSTest version enforces strict type matching between `DataRow` arguments and method parameters.
